snippets.title: Discrete PMF with SciPy
snippets.code:
from scipy import stats

# X ~ Binomial(n=3, p=0.4): number of successes in 3 trials
X = stats.binom(n=3, p=0.4)

pmf_vals = {k: X.pmf(k) for k in range(4)}
print(pmf_vals)          # P(X=k) for k=0,1,2,3
print(sum(pmf_vals.values()))  # 1.0
snippets.explanation: `pmf(k)` returns P(X = k) for discrete distributions. Summing over all support values confirms the PMF totals 1.

snippets.title: Continuous PDF and interval probability
snippets.code:
from scipy import stats

# X ~ Normal(mean=0, std=1)
X = stats.norm(loc=0, scale=1)

# PDF at a point (density, not probability)
print(X.pdf(0))          # ~0.399

# Interval probability via CDF
p = X.cdf(1.96) - X.cdf(-1.96)
print(round(p, 4))       # ~0.95
snippets.explanation: `pdf(x)` gives density; use `cdf` differences for P(a ≤ X ≤ b). Never treat pdf(0) as P(X = 0) for continuous X.

snippets.title: Softmax as a categorical PMF in PyTorch
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F

logits = torch.tensor([2.0, 1.0, 0.1])  # unnormalized scores
probs = F.softmax(logits, dim=0)

print(probs)             # sums to 1.0
print(probs.argmax())    # most likely class index
snippets.explanation: Softmax converts logits to a valid PMF over class labels—a discrete random variable's predicted distribution used in classification and language modeling.
